what_it_does: Acceptance test script for the composable-mind substrate (runtime/minds.py and runtime/cognition). It discovers and resolves registry rows by id and mind:// URI, asserts fail-loud behavior for unknown minds, monkey-patches cognition.resolve_address to simulate the unlanded mind:// seam so traverse returns an ordered Role-shaped cast, checks binding_for_mode, and demonstrates recomposition by editing row files in a temporary directory.
observations: Self-contained runner using PASS/FAIL lists and an ok() helper instead of a framework like pytest.
Monkey-patches cognition.resolve_address with _resolve_with_mind to route mind:// addresses through minds.mind_registry().resolve().
Uses a temporary directory of .py files containing MIND dictionaries to test discovery and row-edit recomposition.
Hardcodes expected role ids mine_exchange and judge_mining and expects cast members to expose .id, .can_fire, and .is_jury attributes.
Comments reference R13, bars 1/2/6, and a meet-point commit cacc9e8b, framing the test as a cross-team seam contract.
standouts: The test simulates an unlanded seam (real mind:// handling in resolve_address) rather than exercising the live integration.
Recomposition is verified by rewriting a row file on disk and rediscovering the registry, claiming zero code change.
The file reads as both an executable acceptance test and a design spec for the composable-mind build.
conventions: Relative sys.path insertion to locate the runtime package.
Inline assertion helper with pass/fail tally and printed ok/FAIL lines.
try/finally to restore the original cognition.resolve_address after the traverse test.
tempfile + shutil.rmtree for isolated row-file experiments.
concerns: Monkeypatching a core resolver makes the test verify logic against a simulated seam, not the real end-to-end.
Hardcoded registry contents and role ids make the test brittle if those rows change.
Writing and importing .py files from a temp directory could leave import side effects or stale modules.
No framework harness means test discovery, fixtures, and reporting are manual.
notes: A fork-side acceptance test that locks expected registry/traverse/cast semantics and marks the real resolve_address mind:// branch as a lead-team meet-point.
questions: What do bars 1, 2, and 6 refer to in the broader plan?
What is the real implementation of the mind:// branch in cognition.resolve_address?
What is run_swarm and how does it consume the cast output?
Where are the first-unit rows (extractor, judge, pair, bind_compose_test) actually defined?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it verifies registry discover/resolve/fail-loud, traverse through resolve_address, binding_for_mode, and recompose-by-row.
apparent_themes: Minds as data-driven, registry-resolvable composable units.
Fail-loud resolution with no default fallback.
Acceptance tests as executable specifications for cross-team seams.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to freeze the contract of a forked composable-mind build, using acceptance tests as a spec and explicitly flagging where the fork must meet the lead team's implementation.
novelty: Demonstrates recomposition by editing a MIND row file on disk and rediscovering the registry, and simulates an unlanded resolver seam via monkeypatch to test traverse logic in isolation.
contribution: An executable acceptance test that captures expected registry semantics, the Role-shaped cast contract, binding_for_mode lookup, and the row-edit recomposition pattern for the composable-mind substrate.
summary_for_embedding: company/tests/minds_acceptance.py is a self-contained acceptance test for the composable-mind registry and traversal that verifies discovery, resolution, fail-loud errors, simulated mind:// traversal into Role-shaped casts, mode binding, and zero-code recomposition by editing row files.
intention_for_embedding: This file was intended to lock the fork-side composable-mind signatures and define the meet-point with the lead team's real resolve_address/run_swarm seams.

symbols.does: Records a pass or fail for a named check and prints an ok/FAIL line.
symbols.description: Test helper that appends the given name to PASS or FAIL based on the boolean cond, then prints an ok or FAIL marker; it only prints the detail string when the check fails.

symbols.does: Monkey-patched resolve_address handler that routes mind:// URLs to the mind registry.
symbols.description: Intercepts calls to cognition.resolve_address for string addresses starting with mind:// and returns minds.mind_registry().resolve(addr); all other addresses are forwarded to the original resolver. It is used to simulate the unlanded seam A branch while testing traverse.
